name: Jay's Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Lakewood BOE plus ~14 public school districts across Monmouth and Ocean Counties; large private (Orthodox Jewish) school contracts; dominant K-12 contractor in Ocean County
recent_news: 2024: Launched real-time bus tracking pilot with Presidential Transportation for Lakewood-area schools; fleet grew from 5 buses (2008) to ~400 vehicles by 2024
fit_notes: Highly attractive Summit tuck-in: independent family-owned, ~400-bus fleet (well above stated 36-employee/$1.65M record - that record likely refers to a satellite/sub-entity), entrenched Ocean County NJ contracts and dominant Orthodox/private-school niche. Founder still active and relatively young, so succession signal is low near-term but scale and density would justify outreach as a strategic regional consolidation play.

name: Guardian School Bus Co
principals: Barbara Larson (CEO per source data); Jeffrey Larson (key contact - likely family)
districts_or_customers: St. Cloud Area School District 742 (25+ year incumbent contractor)
recent_news: No notable M&A or news found; continues longstanding ISD 742 contract relationship
fit_notes: Classic small family-owned tuck-in: ~37 years in business, single anchor district (ISD 742, ~10K students), Larson family principals likely at/near retirement age given founding date. Small fleet but high-quality incumbent contract; would extend Summit footprint into central Minnesota. Worth a confidential approach focused on succession.

name: Clearcut Inc Which Will Do Bus
principals: Ben Artinian (per ClearCut Inc. CA jewelry wholesale entity)
districts_or_customers: None identified - not a school bus operator
website: https://www.clearcutgroup.ca (unrelated Canadian landscaping firm); CA entity is at 12975 Brookprinter Pl, Poway
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET - data hygiene issue. The legal name 'Clearcut Inc. which will do business in California as Clearcut Inc. Wholesale' is a jewelry/precious-stones wholesaler in Poway (per CA SOS / D&B). The website on file (clearcutgroup.ca) is a Toronto-area landscaping firm. No evidence of school bus operations. Drop from buyer list.

name: HT Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Lakewood-area schools (Orthodox Jewish private/yeshiva schools and likely Lakewood BOE routing); intrastate NJ, 100-mile radius
recent_news: Active interstate carrier registered with FMCSA July 2020; no M&A news identified
fit_notes: Small, newer (2020) independent operator in the dense Lakewood NJ market - same yeshiva-driven demand pool as Jay's Bus, Presidential, and many smaller Lakewood contractors. Limited scale (~22 buses) and young founder make succession-driven exit unlikely, but plausible roll-in as part of a Lakewood/Ocean County cluster strategy alongside other NJ tuck-ins.

name: DATTCO School Bus
principals: DeVivo family (Louis & Edward DeVivo founders w/ Mike Tolomea); current leadership Don DeVivo and family
fleet_size_est: ~1,200-1,700 vehicles platform-wide (school bus division is largest segment)
districts_or_customers: 30+ school districts in CT and RI including Bloomfield, Brooklyn, Burrillville, Coventry, Cromwell, East Hartford, Middletown, North Haven, North Smithfield, Plainfield, Plainville, Regional 7/8/13, Smithfield, Thompson, Woodstock; ~140,000 students/day
recent_news: 2024: Celebrated 100th anniversary; continues to operate as the largest private passenger transportation provider in New England with 1,700 vehicles from 30+ locations
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN. DATTCO is the dominant New England school bus / motorcoach platform (~1,200-1,700 vehicles, 140K students/day across CT & RI, plus Megabus and vehicle sales). The Windsor CT address is one of 30+ DATTCO locations, not a standalone target. Third-generation DeVivo family ownership; would be a strategic platform-scale acquisition, not a tuck-in. The 36-employee/$1.5M source record reflects the Windsor depot only. Flag as platform-tier opportunity if/when DeVivo family considers a sale; otherwise exclude from tuck-in pipeline.
